regions.us-ports: Today Miami is SE 11kt with clear skies and less than 10% rain probability, supporting routine embarkation, gangway operations, and shore excursions under light SE winds. Heat Advisory in effect for New Orleans (heat index up to 115°F) and Galveston and Houston (heat index up to 115°F); Extreme Heat Warning in effect for New Orleans (heat index up to 115°F) and Galveston and Houston (heat index up to 115°F). Over the next 24-48 hours Port Everglades and Port Canaveral are forecast SE 11kt and overcast with less than 10% rain probability, and beyond 48 hours Miami and Tampa Bay shift to ENE 14kt with drizzle and less than 10% rain probability.
regions.bahamas-central-caribbean: Today SE 10kt, overcast, less than 10% rain probability, and the light southeasterly wind with low rain probability supports routine embarkation, tender operations and scheduled shore excursions at Nassau, Freeport and Bimini. Over the next 24 to 48 hours ESE 11kt, overcast, less than 10% rain probability will persist, guiding deck-service planning and gangway transfer procedures in Key West, Grand Cayman and Ocho Rios. Beyond 48 hours a shift to E 15kt with thunderstorms and 28% rain probability signals stronger east winds and increased thunderstorm timing for La Romana, Santo Domingo and Samaná, so passengers should expect evolving conditions.
regions.eastern-caribbean: Today PRIORITY ALERT: elevated rain chance (40% rain probability, drizzle) may affect shore excursions and onshore tendering in San Juan, St. Thomas and St. Croix with NE 10kt winds forecast for Day 1. Over the next 24–48 hours thunderstorms (42% rain probability) could affect port calls at St. Maarten and Antigua as ESE 14kt winds and thunderstorm activity are expected on Day 2. Beyond 48 hours San Juan is forecast E 13kt with drizzle and 13% rain probability on Day 3, showing a decline in rain probability compared with earlier days.

regions.lesser-antilles: Today ESE 8kt, drizzle, less than 10% rain probability; light ESE winds and drizzle permit standard embarkation and disembarkation at Bridgetown cruise terminal and routine shore-excursion loading. Day 1 calls for E 9kt, drizzle, less than 10% rain probability and Day 2 E 12kt, drizzle, less than 10% rain probability, with easterly winds likely to influence tendering and pier approaches at St. Lucia, Martinique and Dominica. By Day 3 ENE 13kt, drizzle, 15% rain probability signals an ENE wind shift and modest speed increase that may necessitate adjustments to anchoring and upper-deck guest activities for Antigua, St. Kitts and St. Maarten.

regions.los-angeles: Today SSW 7kt with clear skies and less than 10% rain probability supports smooth embarkation and disembarkation at Los Angeles / San Pedro and efficient outdoor shore excursions from Long Beach. Over the next 24 to 48 hours WSW 13kt (Day 1) and W 12kt (Day 2) with overcast and less than 10% rain probability will require tendering and gangway adjustments at Catalina Island and Marina del Rey while Los Angeles / San Pedro and Long Beach see similar overcast conditions. Beyond 48 hours SW 9kt with clear skies and less than 10% rain probability should ease deck operations and allow rescheduling of open-deck services and exterior maintenance.

Beyond 48 hours Day 3 WSW 8kt, overcast and less than 10% rain probability indicates increased cloud cover that may curb sun-dependent excursions and require deck lighting checks at Ensenada, Punta Banda and Islas Todos Santos.
